title: AAR: Academic & Mental Attributes System
scope: Added a comprehensive academic and mental attributes system to Viperball's recruiting engine. This makes recruiting feel like a real marketplace where academic eligibility gates who can go where, and mental traits (field intelligence, coachability) determine how well recruits develop once they arrive.

rationale.item: No other college sports sim does this well. Most games treat academics as a binary pass/fail or ignore it entirely.
Stanford's 3.85 median GPA / 1480 median VAT makes it impossible to offer a 2.1 GPA recruit; this distinction was missing

delegation_events.decision: Separate Field Intelligence from Awareness because they develop differently and are scouted differently
delegation_events.quote: A quarterback who can read a Cover 2 shell (field IQ) is different from one who has perfect footwork (awareness/technique). You want both, but they develop differently and are scouted differently.

delegation_events.decision: Coachability is independent of skill — a personality trait, not a skill
delegation_events.quote: It's a personality trait, not a skill. Some elite athletes are uncoachable — they've always been the best and never learned to take instruction.

delegation_events.decision: Bell curves with outliers instead of flat distributions
delegation_events.quote: Reality. Most recruits cluster around the middle. The outliers (1500+ VAT, sub-2.0 GPA) are rare and notable.

delegation_events.decision: Compute tier from medians instead of static labels for moddability
delegation_events.quote: Moddability. You can change a school's median_gpa in the JSON and the tier updates automatically.

actions.description: Recruit dataclass: 4 new attributes (Field Intelligence, Coachability, GPA, VAT Score), academic risk computed field, eligibility gating
School academic profiles: 205 team JSON files updated with median_gpa, median_vat, academic_tier
Academic Profile section on college team page
Recruit profile: GPA, VAT, Field IQ, Coachability, academic risk flags display

non_actions.reason: You can offer $500K to a kid but if they can't get into Stanford, they can't get into Stanford.

open_hooks: Dynasty mode can eventually drift school academics based on program success, coaching changes, or conference realignment since tier is computed from median values
APR metric (compute_team_apr()) tracks team-wide academic health — below 92.5% = NCAA penalty zone
section_headers_original: Why separate Field Intelligence from Awareness?
Why bell curves with outliers instead of flat distributions?
Why compute tier from medians instead of static labels?
unmapped_content: Date in subtitle 'April 2026' — no specific day given. Filename says 2026-04-07, not confirmed in document body. Using null for date with parser note. Academic tier table showing 5 tiers (Elite/High/Above Average/Standard/Flexible) with example schools, GPA ranges, VAT ranges, and admissions flex. Interaction section: Program Changer coaches effectively bypass academic barriers; NIL doesn't override academics; Transfer portal uses same eligibility check; APR metric tracks team academic health.
